main_paper.raw: {"methods":["Pipeline to generate a synthetic video dataset for video face re-aging (using StyleGAN for face synthesis, SAM for age transformation, OSFV for pose/expression variation, FILM for motion interpolation)","Baseline network architecture for video re-aging using recurrent blocks with U-Net, 2D and 3D discriminators","Novel evaluation metrics for temporal consistency: Temporal Regional Wrinkle Consistency (TRWC) and Temporally Age Preservation"],"problems":["Lack of paired video datasets for face re-aging with temporal consistency in identity and age","Existing methods process images individually, leading to poor temporal consistency in videos","Existing metrics are not suitable for evaluating temporal consistency in video face re-aging"],"datasets":["Proposed synthetic video face re-aging dataset (introduced in this paper)","VFHQ","CelebV-HQ"],"metrics":["Temporal Regional Wrinkle Consistency (TRWC)","Temporally Age Preservation","User study preference"],"results":[{"metric":"User study preference (temporal consistency, older direction)","value":"48.1%"},{"metric":"User study preference (temporal consistency, younger direction)","value":"39.3%"}],"novelty_claims":["Introduction of a synthetic video dataset for face re-aging with paired data across ages, poses, and expressions","Development of a baseline architecture leveraging temporal coherence for video face re-aging","Proposal of novel metrics (TRWC and Temporally Age Preservation) specifically for evaluating temporal consistency in video re-aging","Demonstration that the proposed method outperforms existing approaches in both age transformation accuracy and temporal consistency on public datasets"]}

methods: Pipeline to generate a synthetic video dataset for video face re-aging (using StyleGAN for face synthesis, SAM for age transformation, OSFV for pose/expression variation, FILM for motion interpolation)
Baseline network architecture for video re-aging using recurrent blocks with U-Net, 2D and 3D discriminators
Novel evaluation metrics for temporal consistency: Temporal Regional Wrinkle Consistency (TRWC) and Temporally Age Preservation
problems: Lack of paired video datasets for face re-aging with temporal consistency in identity and age
Existing methods process images individually, leading to poor temporal consistency in videos
Existing metrics are not suitable for evaluating temporal consistency in video face re-aging
datasets: Proposed synthetic video face re-aging dataset (introduced in this paper)
VFHQ
CelebV-HQ

results.metric: User study preference (temporal consistency, older direction)
User study preference (temporal consistency, younger direction)

novelty_claims: Introduction of a synthetic video dataset for face re-aging with paired data across ages, poses, and expressions
Development of a baseline architecture leveraging temporal coherence for video face re-aging
Proposal of novel metrics (TRWC and Temporally Age Preservation) specifically for evaluating temporal consistency in video re-aging
Demonstration that the proposed method outperforms existing approaches in both age transformation accuracy and temporal consistency on public datasets

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["Pipeline to generate a synthetic video dataset for face re-aging using StyleGAN, SAM, OSFV, and FILM","Baseline network architecture with recurrent blocks and U-Net modules for video re-aging","Fusion-based approach leveraging concatenated inputs for temporal consistency","Video discriminator with 3D convolutional layers for realism and natural motion","Novel evaluation metrics: Temporal Regional Wrinkle Consistency (TRWC) and Temporally Age Preservation"],"problems":["Lack of paired video datasets for face re-aging with temporal consistency in identity and age","Existing methods process images individually, leading to poor temporal consistency in videos","Existing video facial attribute manipulation methods fail in precise age transformation","Lack of suitable metrics for evaluating temporal consistency in video face re-aging"],"datasets":["Proposed synthetic video face re-aging dataset (introduced in this paper)","VFHQ","CelebA-HQ"],"metrics":["Temporal Regional Wrinkle Consistency (TRWC)","Temporally Age Preservation","User study preference"],"results":[{"metric":"User study preference (temporal consistency, older direction)","value":"48.1% preferred the proposed method"},{"metric":"User study preference (temporal consistency, younger direction)","value":"39.3% preferred the proposed method"}],"novelty_claims":["First pipeline to generate a synthetic video dataset specifically for video face re-aging, with paired videos across ages, poses, and expressions","Baseline network architecture designed for video re-aging using recurrent blocks, U-Net modules, and both 2D and 3D discriminators","Development of novel metrics (TRWC and Temporally Age Preservation) for evaluating temporal consistency in video re-aging","Demonstrated superior performance in age transformation accuracy and temporal consistency compared to state-of-the-art methods"]}

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["Re-Aging GAN++ (RAGAN++): a unified GAN-based framework for facial age transformation in videos","Multi-scale personalized age feature modulation to represent target age attributes","Encoder with Gaussian smoothing at each scale to ensure seamless frame-to-frame transitions and handle inter-frame variations"],"problems":["Facial age transformation in videos with spatial and temporal consistency","Scarcity of video datasets with individuals across multiple age groups","Preserving facial identity, expression, and pose during age transformation in videos"],"datasets":[],"metrics":[],"results":[],"novelty_claims":["Proposes a GAN-based model (RAGAN++) that performs facial age transformation in videos using only still image training data","Introduces multi-scale personalized age feature modulation for accurate age attribute depiction","Applies Gaussian smoothing at each scale in the encoder to ensure temporally consistent transitions between video frames","Achieves spatio-temporal consistency in facial identity, expression, and pose during age transformation in videos"]}

methods: Systematic procedure for constructing a large-scale, high-quality video facial attributes dataset (CelebV-HQ), including: large-scale Internet query generation, automatic pre-processing pipeline (face detection, alignment, quality filtering), and a systematic manual annotation process (annotator training, automatic judgment, quality check)
Comprehensive statistical analysis of dataset diversity and quality (age, ethnicity, brightness stability, motion smoothness, head pose diversity, data quality)

Evaluation of representative baselines for unconditional video generation (using state-of-the-art video GANs) and video facial attribute editing (using temporally constrained image-to-image baselines)
problems: Lack of a large-scale, high-quality video dataset with diverse facial attribute annotations for face-related video research

Challenges in constructing video datasets: scale (temporal consistency, high-resolution, full-head), quality (fidelity, resolution, diversity), and comprehensive attribute annotation (appearance, action, emotion)
datasets: CelebV-HQ (proposed)
CelebA
CelebA-HQ
FFHQ
VoxCeleb2

results.metric: Number of video clips in CelebV-HQ

results.value: at least 512x512

results.metric: Temporal consistency improvement in video facial attribute editing
results.value: Remarkable improvement over image-based methods (qualitative claim)

novelty_claims: CelebV-HQ is the first large-scale, high-quality face video dataset with diverse, manually annotated facial attributes, filling the gap in video modality for facial attribute research.
CelebV-HQ provides comprehensive attribute coverage (appearance, action, emotion) with 83 manually labeled attributes.
The dataset construction process ensures high fidelity, diversity, and temporal coherence, surpassing existing image and video datasets in quality and diversity.
CelebV-HQ enables and empirically improves tasks such as unconditional video generation and video facial attribute editing, especially in temporal consistency.

methods: DiffAge3D: a 3D-aware diffusion-based face aging framework
3D-aware aging dataset generation pipeline using EG3D (3D GAN) and CLIP for text-image alignment
Training on data sampled from the 3D GAN latent space without inversion
Viewpoint-aware diffusion-based aging model to control camera pose and facial age
Fine-tuning Zero123 on EG3D-generated data and combining with ControlNet for viewpoint manipulation
Incorporation of motion modules with temporal attention layers for smooth viewpoint transitions
problems: Lack of 3D-aware face aging methods (existing methods are limited to 2D)
Difficulty in faithful aging, identity preservation, and fine detail retention in face aging
Scarcity of large-scale, paired, and multiview aging datasets
Inability of existing methods to control camera viewpoint and age separately
Quality and information loss due to inversion bottlenecks in GAN-based methods
datasets: Generated 3D-aware aging dataset using EG3D and CLIP (proposed in this paper)
FFHQ (used in related works, but not as main dataset in this work)

results.value: DiffAge3D outperforms existing methods (quantitative results not specified in provided text)
DiffAge3D achieves superior multiview-consistent aging compared to prior methods (quantitative results not specified in provided text)
DiffAge3D preserves fine details better than state-of-the-art (qualitative and quantitative evidence claimed)

novelty_claims: First 3D-aware face aging framework that models aging and camera pose separately using a single image and target age
Robust 3D-aware aging dataset generation pipeline using EG3D and CLIP, without inversion bottleneck, enabling generation of multiview consistent aged faces
Viewpoint-aware diffusion-based aging model that can control both age and camera pose, preserving identity and background
Incorporation of motion modules with temporal attention for smooth and consistent viewpoint transitions
Extensive experiments show superior performance over state-of-the-art in aging accuracy and 3D consistency

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["ToonAging: a one-stage method that combines face re-aging and portrait style transfer in a single generative step","Latent fusion approach: merging latent vectors from SAM (for re-aging) and DualStyleGAN (for style transfer) in the W+ space of StyleGAN","Exemplar-based style transfer: using example images for flexible style transfer without domain-level fine-tuning"],"problems":["Lack of methods for face re-aging in non-photorealistic (NPR) images such as comics, illustrations, and animations","Sequential application of re-aging and style transfer leads to artifacts and loss of facial attributes due to domain discrepancies","Existing methods require paired datasets or domain-level fine-tuning, limiting flexibility and generalization"],"datasets":["FFHQ (Flickr-Faces-HQ, as referenced by StyleGAN training)","Artistic portrait datasets (implied by use of DualStyleGAN and NPR images, though not explicitly named)"],"metrics":["Qualitative visual comparison","Attribute preservation (e.g., facial expression, wrinkles)","Style transfer fidelity"],"results":[],"novelty_claims":["First method to combine face re-aging and artistic portrait style transfer in a single generative step (one-stage method)","Latent fusion of distinct networks (SAM and DualStyleGAN) in the W+ space for seamless merging of aging and style attributes","Exemplar-based approach allows style transfer to any domain without additional fine-tuning or training","Precise and independent control over aging and style attributes, overcoming limitations of sequential and domain-level approaches"]}

methods: ToonAging: a one-stage method that combines face re-aging and portrait style transfer in a single generative step
Latent fusion approach: merging latent vectors from SAM (for re-aging) and DualStyleGAN (for style transfer) in the W+ space of StyleGAN
Exemplar-based style transfer: using example images for flexible style transfer without domain-level fine-tuning
problems: Lack of methods for face re-aging in non-photorealistic (NPR) images such as comics, illustrations, and animations
Sequential application of re-aging and style transfer leads to artifacts and loss of facial attributes due to domain discrepancies
Existing methods require paired datasets or domain-level fine-tuning, limiting flexibility and generalization
datasets: FFHQ (Flickr-Faces-HQ, as referenced by StyleGAN training)
Artistic portrait datasets (implied by use of DualStyleGAN and NPR images, though not explicitly named)
novelty_claims: First method to combine face re-aging and artistic portrait style transfer in a single generative step (one-stage method)
Latent fusion of distinct networks (SAM and DualStyleGAN) in the W+ space for seamless merging of aging and style attributes
Exemplar-based approach allows style transfer to any domain without additional fine-tuning or training
Precise and independent control over aging and style attributes, overcoming limitations of sequential and domain-level approaches

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["GAN-based identity-preserving aging method (M2aging)","High-fidelity Multi-scale Attention (HiMA) module","Multi-attribution Fusion Modulator (MFM) for fusing age, gender, and race attributes"],"problems":["Insufficient depiction of the real aging process when only age is used as a condition for face aging","Maintaining identity information during face age transformation","Controlling the aging process with multiple attributes (age, gender, race)"],"datasets":[],"metrics":["Identity preservation","Age accuracy/conversion","Attribution preservation (age, gender, race)"],"results":[],"novelty_claims":["Proposes a GAN-based method (M2aging) that fuses multiple attributions (age, gender, race) for face aging, rather than using only age.","Introduces a High-fidelity Multi-scale Attention (HiMA) module to capture minute and local features at different scales for better identity preservation.","Proposes a Multi-attribution Fusion Modulator (MFM) to control the aging process by fusing multiple attributes without extra classifiers or training losses.","Demonstrates that the proposed method outperforms state-of-the-art techniques in identity preservation and age conversion accuracy."]}

methods: GAN-based identity-preserving aging method (M2aging)
High-fidelity Multi-scale Attention (HiMA) module
Multi-attribution Fusion Modulator (MFM) for fusing age, gender, and race attributes
problems: Insufficient depiction of the real aging process when only age is used as a condition for face aging
Maintaining identity information during face age transformation
Controlling the aging process with multiple attributes (age, gender, race)
novelty_claims: Proposes a GAN-based method (M2aging) that fuses multiple attributions (age, gender, race) for face aging, rather than using only age.
Introduces a High-fidelity Multi-scale Attention (HiMA) module to capture minute and local features at different scales for better identity preservation.
Proposes a Multi-attribution Fusion Modulator (MFM) to control the aging process by fusing multiple attributes without extra classifiers or training losses.
Demonstrates that the proposed method outperforms state-of-the-art techniques in identity preservation and age conversion accuracy.

methods: Unified network structure embedding a linear age estimator into a GAN-based model
Joint training of the embedded age estimator with encoder and decoder
Synthesis of personalized target age embedding by combining personalized residual age embedding and exemplar-face aging basis

problems: Lack of fine-controlled continuous face aging synthesis in existing methods

results.metric: Age estimation accuracy (alignment with target age)

novelty_claims: Proposes a novel method to self-estimate continuous age embeddings and derive personalized age embeddings for face aging by jointly training an age estimator with the generator.
Achieves continuous aging synthesis without manual definition of age groups, enabling fine-grained aging control.
Demonstrates better preservation of personalized information and more accurate aging control than previous methods.
The self-estimated personalized age embedding approach can be generalized to other conditional image-to-image translation tasks involving continuous conditions.

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["Style-based regression model for age transformation (SAM - Style-based Age Manipulation)","Image-to-image translation using an encoder to map real facial images into Demonstrates, through extensive qualitative and quantitative experiments, that the method outperforms state-of-the-art aging methods in generating plausible and diverse aging patterns.
